source_pr.title: plugin, tests, tools, tidb: unify go modules
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed:

- Unified the go modules because:

1. Previously it had 4 modules:
```
$ find . -iname "go.mod"
./go.mod
./tools/check/go.mod
./tests/globalkilltest/go.mod
./tests/graceshutdown/go.mod
```

So after checking if they could be unified, I didn't find anything conflicting and it seems totally fine to keep one go.{mod,sum} for the project.

Also, `go.mod` didn't have consistent module paths. Example:
```
$ cat tests/graceshutdown/go.mod | head -1

module graceshutdown
```

```
$ cat tests/globalkilltest/go.mod | head -1

module github.com/pingcap/tests/globalkilltest
```
Also, do notice that `tidb` is missing from `github.com/pingcap/tests/globalkilltest`. It should be actually `github.com/pingcap/tidb/tests/globalkilltest`

2. Only one problem was there in `tests/graceshutdown/graceshutdown_test.go` that was not letting me create a single module because there was a usage of `log.Warnf`:
```go
log.Warnf("close db failed, retry count %d err %v", i, err)
```
which I'm assuming is the only reason it did have an independent `go.mod` file because it was using an older version of the package `github.com/pingcap/log`:
```
github.com/pingcap/log v0.0.0-20191012051959-b742a5d432e9 // used by tests/graceshutdown
```
which had the `log.Warnf` method. But in the newer version, it was removed and it's currently used by `github.com/pingcap/tidb`. Although, it not a good practice to have such breaking changes without upgrading the major version of the package (maybe I'll create an issue for `github.com/pingcap/log`).

So, to eliminate the usage of the older package, the line could be replace by:
```
- log.Warnf("close db failed, retry count %d err %v", i, err)
+ log.Warn("close db failed,", zap.Int("retry count", i), zap.Error(err))
```
which is the same and lets us use the new log package used by `tidb`.

3. Based on Go's official doc a **package** is defined as:
> At least one file with the .go extension must be present in a directory for it to be considered a package. (Ref: https://golang.org/ref/mod#resolve-pkg-mod)

But tools/check didn't have any *.go files and hence no need to create a separate module for that.

So, having a single go.mod for the project is totally fine based on the above-mentioned points. 

- Rename test package

Any package with the name `package main` requires the package to have a `main` function (`func main()`) but `github.com/pingcap/tidb/plugin/conn_ip_example` didn't have it. So renamed the package to `test`. A very minor change.


### Related changes

- PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

Tests
- No code

### Release note
- Unify Go modules for github.com/pingcap/tidb

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to nondeterministic behavior that was not fully captured in the extracted evidence. This PR stabilizes it by: gopkg.in/alecthomas/kingpin.v3-unstable v3.0.0-20180810215634-df19058c872c/go.mod h1:3HH7i1SgMqlzxCcBmUHW657sD4Kvv9sC3HpL3YukzwA=.

evidence: files: go.mod, go.sum, owner/fail_test.go, plugin/conn_ip_example/conn_ip_example.go, plugin/conn_ip_example/conn_ip_example_test.go, tests/globalkilltest/go.sum, tools/check/go.mod, tools/check/go.sum
